items.title: Hindu bias in India's 'secular' constitution: Probing flaws in the instruments of governance
items.summary: Examines the constitutional framework of India and analyzes biases in governance structures despite secular principles

items.title: The Political Economy of the Cycles of Violence and Non-violence in the Sikh Struggle for Identity and Political Power: implications for Indian federalism
items.summary: Analyzes the Sikh political movement and its implications for federal structures and minority rights in India

items.title: The resurgence of Bhindranwale's image in contemporary Punjab
items.summary: Discusses the political and cultural significance of religious leadership in Punjab's contemporary context

items.title: Austerity, welfare state and eco-socialism: With special reference to the United Kingdom
items.summary: Analyzes the impact of austerity policies on welfare systems and explores eco-socialist alternatives

items.title: Federalism and the Accommodation of Distinct Groups: A Comparative Study of the Indian and Pakistani Experiences
items.summary: Comparative analysis of federal systems and minority accommodation in South Asian contexts

items.title: Culture and the Economy: Understanding the Dynamics of Globalization
items.summary: Explores the relationship between cultural factors and economic development in the context of globalization
